sections.paragraph:
( Num 17:2) fut. יִקְדַּשׁ-

(1) to be pure, clean, prop. used of physical purity and cleanliness (see Hithp. No. 1, and adj. קָדֹושׁ); hence-

(2) to be holy, sacred (so in all the cogn. languages, Arab. قدس id.)-(a) used of a man who devotes himself to any God, and therefore accounts himself more holy than the common people. Isa 65:5, קְדַשְׁתִּיךָ “I am holy unto thee,” for קָדַשְׁתִּי לְךָ; used of those who were consecrated by touching sacred things, Exo 29:37 30:29 Lev 6:11, 20 .-(b) used of things destined for holy worship, Num 17:2, Num 17:3 Exo 29:21 or which were consecrated by touching holy things, 1Sa 21:6 Hag 2:12 or which were devoted to the sacred treasury, Deu 22:9.

Niphal-

(1) to be regarded as holy; to be hallowed (as of God), followed by בְּ Levit. 10:3 22:32 also to shew oneself holy in any thing, either by bestowing favours, Eze 20:41 28:25 36:23 38:16 39:27 or in inflicting punishments, Eze 28:22 Num 20:13 compare Isa 5:16.

(2) to be consecrated (used of the holy tent), Exo 29:43.

Piel קִדַּשׁ-

(1) to regard any one as holy, as God, Deu 32:51, a priest, Lev 21:8 the sabbath, Exo 20:8.

(2) to declare holy (used of God), e.g. the sabbath, Gen 2:3 the people, Lev 20:8 21:8 also to institute any thing sacred, as a fast, Joe 1:14 2:15 (to which answers קָרָא), a general assembly, 2Ki 10:20.

(3) to consecrate a priest, Exo 28:41 29:1 1Sa 7:1 the altar, the temple, Exo 29:36 1Ki 8:64 the people of Israel, Exo 19:10, 14 Exo 19:14 Jos 7:13 a new building, Neh 3:1 a mountain (by separating it from profane things), Exo 19:23. Hence to inaugurate with holy rites, as a sacrifice, Exo 12:2 soldiers for battle, Jerem. 51:27 . Compare Hiphil. קִדַּשׁ מִלְחָמָה to consecrate war, to inaugurate (with sacred rites), compare Psa 110:3 1Sa 7:9, 10 1Sa 7:10 Joel 4:9 Jerem. 6:4 Mic 3:5.

Pual, part. consecrated, used of priests and holy things, Eze 48:11 2Ch 26:18 31:6 . Isa 13:3, מְקֻדָּשַׁי “my consecrated ones,” i.e. soldiers whom I myself have inaugurated for war, comp. Jer 51:27.

Hiphil-

(1) i.q. Piel No. 1, Isa 8:13 29:23 Num 20:12.

(2) i.q. Piel No. 2, to declare any one holy, Jer 1:5 1Ki 9:3.

(3) i.q. Piel No. 3, to consecrate to God, Lev 27:14, seqq., Jud. 17:3 2Sa 8:11.

Hithpael-

(1) to purify, to make oneself clean (by holy washings and lustrations). 2Sa 11:4, וְהִיא מִתְקַדֶּשֶׁת מִטֻּמְאָתָהּ “and she purified herself from her uncleanness;” Exo 19:22 2Ch 5:11 29:15 comp. Kal No. 1.

(2) to shew oneself holy, Eze 38:23.

(3) to be celebrated, as a feast, Isa 30:29.

Derivatives, קָדֵשׁ, קֶדֶשׁ, קֹדֶשׁ, קַדִּישׁ, קָדֹושׁ, מִקְדָּשׁ.

קדש

m.-

(1) a sodomite, pr. consecrated, sc. to Astarte or Venus, and prostituting himself in her honour, Deu 23:18 1Ki 14:24 15:12 22:47 Job 36:14. Fem. קְדֵשָׁה consecrated (to Venus), hence a harlot, Gen 38:21, 22 Gen 38:22 Deu 23:18 Hos 4:14. As to the libidinous worship of Venus amongst the Babylonians, see Lucian, De Dea Syra; compare Num 25:1, seqq.

(2) קָדֵשׁ Gen 14:7 16:14 and fully קָדֵשׁ בַּרְנֵעַ Num 34:4 Deu 1:2, 19 2:14 [Kadesh, Kadeshbarnea], pr.n. of a town in the desert to the south of Palestine, see Relandi Palæstina, p. 114. Hence מִדְבַּר קָדֵשׁ Psa 29:8.
